channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How to Hire Generative AI Developers
item.description: The rapid evolution of artificial intelligence has transformed the way businesses operate, innovate, and compete. Among the most impactful advancements is generative AI, which enables machines to...

item.title: The IRS Wants Smarter Audits. Palantir Could Help Decide Who Gets Flagged
item.description: Documents show the tax agency is testing a Palantir tool to surface “highest-value” audit and investigation targets from a maze of legacy systems.

item.title: Return to Office Is Not a Productivity Strategy: What Actually Makes Developers Effective in 2026
item.description: A friend of mine, a staff engineer at a company you have heard of, got the email in January. Five days a week in the office starting March 1st. No exceptions. No negotiation. He had been remote...

item.title: You're Looking at the Wrong Pretext Demo
item.description: Pretext, a new JavaScript library from Cheng Lou, crossed 7,000 GitHub stars in its first three days. If you've been anywhere near the frontend engineering circles in that time, you've seen the...

item.title: My Learning Journey as a Developer
item.description: Two months ago, I started this learning journey with a mix of anxiety and determination. At the time, I relied heavily on tutorials and artificial intelligence. If I couldn’t find a guide that...

item.title: Understanding Data Modeling in Power BI
item.description: Data modeling is the invisible foundation underneath every Power BI report. Get it right, and your visuals are fast, accurate, and easy to maintain. Get it wrong, and you spend hours debugging...

item.title: Understanding Data Modelling in Power BI: Joins, Relationships, and Schemas Explained
item.description: Data modeling is like building a well-organized library 📚. It helps us connect different pieces of information in a way that makes analysis easy and meaningful. In Power BI, data modeling is the...

item.title: Building a PostgreSQL Observability Stack with Docker, Grafana, and Prometheus
item.description: How to monitor your PostgreSQL database with metrics collection, log aggregation, and beautiful dashboards Introduction If you're running PostgreSQL in production, you need to know what's...

item.description: Part 3: The Scoring Engine — How a Robot Selects the Perfect Viewpoint In the previous post, we discussed the "Single Camera + 12 Virtual Nodes" strategy to overcome simulation lag. But with 4...

item.title: I Had 400+ Bookmarks and Zero Organization. Here's What Fixed It.
item.description: I Had 400+ Bookmarks and Zero Organization. Here's What Fixed It. Last month I counted my Chrome bookmarks. 437 items spread across 12 folders with names like "Useful," "Check Later," and my...

item.title: rails-local-ci: Essential Local CI for Older Rails Apps
item.description: Rails 8.1 shipped a genuinely useful feature: a standardized bin/ci script backed by ActiveSupport::ContinuousIntegration. Run your full test suite, linter, and security checks locally before...

item.title: Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones
item.description: After operating in secrecy for years, a startup company called R3 Bio, in Richmond, California, suddenly shared details about its work last week—saying it had raised money to create nonsentient...

item.title: Three China-Linked Clusters Target Southeast Asian Government in 2025 Cyber Campaign
item.description: Three threat activity clusters aligned with China have targeted a government organization in Southeast Asia as part of what has been described as a "complex and well-resourced operation." The...

item.title: Why OpenAI really shut down Sora
item.description: OpenAI's decision last week to shut down Sora, its AI video-generation tool, just six months after releasing it to the public raised immediate suspicions. The app had invited users to upload their...

item.title: All the latest in AI ‘music’
item.description: AI has touched every part of the music industry, from sample sourcing and demo recording, to serving up digital liner notes and building playlists. There are technical and legal challenges, fierce...

item.title: Helping disaster response teams turn AI into action across Asia
item.description: AI for Disaster Response in Asia: OpenAI Workshop with Gates Foundation

item.title: Bluesky’s new app is an AI for customizing your feed
item.description: The latest app from the team behind Bluesky is Attie, an AI assistant that lets you build your own algorithm. At the Atmosphere conference, Bluesky's former CEO, Jay Graber, and CTO Paul Frazee,...

item.title: Red Rooms makes online poker as thrilling as its serial killer
item.description: It's rare for a movie to get technology right. And it's even rarer for that movie to be a thriller or horror, where realism takes a backseat to scares and tension. But Red Rooms mostly gets it....

item.title: The best deals we’ve found from Amazon’s Big Spring Sale (so far)
item.description: Amazon loves to manufacture an event. March is historically a dry spell for deals; however, with Amazon’s third annual Big Spring Sale, which runs through March 31st, the retail behemoth is hoping...

item.title: Sora’s shutdown could be a reality check moment for AI video
item.description: Is this just normal corporate strategy, or are we about to see a broader pullback on AI-generated video?

item.title: Can my favorite Game Boy gadget tell fake cartridges from real?
item.description: The $50 Epilogue GB Operator has a brand-new trick up its sleeve. In addition to backing up Game Boy, Game Boy Color and Game Boy Advance cartridges and saves to a PC (and writing saves back to...

item.title: There is no ethical consumption of HBO’s Harry Potter series
item.description: In the coming years, HBO wants its new Harry Potter series to become "the streaming event of the decade" as it adapts each of the franchise's seven original books. The show could very well become...

item.title: Amazon’s Big Spring Sale is a great time to get a fancy, AI-powered bird feeder
item.description: Bird feeders have come a long way since their inception in the early 1900s. These days, it’s not all that surprising to see feeders kitted out with cameras and solar roofs, two marquee features...

item.title: The Macintosh changed computers forever
item.description: Apple's most legendary computer has two legacies: there's the computer itself, and there's the commercial. That commercial. Only a couple of days before Steve Jobs debuted the computer that would...

item.title: Apple’s long, bitter App Store antitrust war
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the legal travails of Big Tech, follow Adi Robertson. The Stepback arrives in our...

item.title: Bluesky leans into AI with Attie, an app for building custom feeds
item.description: Bluesky’s new app Attie uses AI to help people build custom feeds the open social networking protocol atproto.

item.title: Stanford study outlines dangers of asking AI chatbots for personal advice
item.description: While there’s been plenty of debate about AI sycophancy, a new study by Stanford computer scientists attempts to measure how harmful that tendency might be.

item.title: Suno leans into customization with v5.5
item.description: Suno just released one of its biggest updates yet with v5.5 of its AI music model. Where previous updates focused mostly on improving fidelity and creating more natural vocals, v5.5 is about...

item.title: Elon Musk’s last co-founder reportedly leaves xAI
item.description: All but two of Musk's 11 xAI co-founders departed before this week.

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Breach FBI Director’s Personal Email, Hit Stryker With Wiper Attack
item.description: Threat actors with ties to Iran successfully broke into the personal email account of Kash Patel, the director of the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), and leaked a cache of photos and...
